item_name: Hard Paywall at Onboarding Climax (incl. Toggle-Paywall Ban)
definition_origin: The 'hard paywall' pattern (no skip, no close, no continue-free path) was popularized 2018-2022 by RevenueCat / Adapty / Superwall analyses of top-grossing iOS subscription apps (Cal AI, Noom, Headway, Blinkist). The 'onboarding climax' placement was crystallized in Mau Baron's 2024-2025 Starter Story / YouTube playbook ($150K/mo Prayer Lock) and Adapty's 2026 High-Performing Paywall report. The 2026-01 Apple App Review Guideline 3.1.2 update — formally banning the 'toggle paywall' (free-trial toggle that auto-rolls into paid sub) — is the regulatory pivot that re-architected the climax paywall in 2026.
core_mechanism: The mechanism stacks four cognitive levers: (1) Sunk-cost fallacy + IKEA effect — the user has invested 5-15 minutes answering questions and helping build their personalized plan; abandoning now feels wasteful. (2) Peak-end engineering — the AI plan reveal at step N-1 creates the peak emotion; the paywall is fired during that peak before reflection re-engages prefrontal deliberation. (3) Endowment — the user already has 'their' plan/streak/profile; the paywall frames payment as protecting what is theirs, not buying something new. (4) Reciprocity — the long onboarding has 'given' personalization, social proof, demos; conversion feels like a fair exchange. The hard variant (no close button, no 'maybe later') removes the reflective off-ramp; the soft variant (X close, see-it-once) preserves it. RevenueCat 2025: hard paywalls produce 21% higher LTV (because committed users self-select), but soft paywalls convert ~50% better at first impression — the LTV-vs-volume tradeoff is the strategic call.
neuroscience_correlate: Mesolimbic dopamine engagement during AI plan reveal (peak) carries forward into paywall — the user is in approach-motivation state, not deliberation. Loss aversion (amygdala / insula) dominates: 'losing my plan' weighs ~2.25x larger than 'gaining a $9.99/mo cost' (Kahneman 1979 prospect theory). Hard paywall removal of close button suppresses dorsolateral prefrontal cortex (DLPFC) deliberation pathway by removing the choice surface itself.
empirical_evidence: RevenueCat State of Subscription Apps 2025 (analysis of 50,000+ apps, 30,000+ paywalls): hard paywalls generate 21% higher 12-month LTV vs soft paywalls; soft paywalls convert ~50% better on first-view but lose 30%+ of those users to churn. RevenueCat 2026 update: 90% of all trial starts happen on Day 0 (the onboarding session), making the climax paywall the single highest-leverage surface in the app. Adapty 2026 High-Performing Paywall report (15B+ events, 20M+ paywall views): onboarding-placed paywall converts at 1.35% (highest of any placement, vs 0.4% home-screen, 0.6% feature-gated). Subscription Insider 2025 (RevenueCat data): top quartile of apps run 14.7 paywall experiments per year. PDF Deep Dive Into App Onboarding (citation 67): 'The highest volume of subscription conversions occurs during the initial onboarding session.' Apple Developer release (Dec 2025 / Jan 2026): Guideline 3.1.2 explicitly prohibits subscription paywalls where the user 'has not made a clear, affirmative choice between starting a free trial that converts to paid and selecting a paid option.' RevenueCat blog 'R.I.P. Toggle Paywall, We Hardly Knew Ye' (Jan 2026) and Adapty 'Your Toggle Paywall Is About To Get Rejected' (Jan 2026) both document the rejection wave; RevenueCat reports rejection within 24-72 hours of submission for any toggle paywall as of 2026-01-15.
measurable_outcome_metric: trial_started_rate (paywall_view → trial_started), paywall_conversion_rate, paywall_dismissed_rate, paywall_to_paid_rate (Day 7+), 12mo cohort LTV, paywall_to_uninstall_rate (D1).
conversion_lift_benchmark: 1.35% trial conversion at onboarding paywall (Adapty 2026, highest of any placement). Hard paywall = 21% higher LTV vs soft (RevenueCat 2025). Top performers running 14.7 experiments/yr capture up to 40x revenue gap vs non-testing teams (RevenueCat 2026). 90% of trial starts on Day 0 (Adapty 2026).
engineering_tactics: RevenueCat Paywalls SDK (Paywall Editor v2, 2025+): use Template 5 (Modal Hard Paywall) with custom_data.show_close_button = false, dismissible_after_seconds = null; bind to Offering with annual_default_selected. Superwall paywall config (campaign rule: trigger = onboarding_completed, audience = first_session, presentation = on_present_skip_if_no_paywall = false). Adapty Placement: paywall_id = 'onboarding_climax', show_close_button = false, fallback_paywall_id = 'onboarding_climax_offline' (offline JSON cached at install). Plan structure: 2-3 SKUs (annual w/3-day-trial as default 'most popular', monthly, optional lifetime). NO toggle paywall (banned 2026-01) — use side-by-side cards with per-card 'Start free trial' CTA OR explicit timeline transparency: 'Day 1 today: free. Day 4: $79.99/year billed. Cancel anytime.' Implementation pattern (React Native + RevenueCat): import { useCustomerInfo, Paywall } from 'react-native-purchases-ui'; const { customerInfo } = useCustomerInfo(); <Paywall offering={offering} onDismiss={null} onPurchaseCompleted={(p) => navigation.replace('home')} />. Server-side: log paywall_view, paywall_close_attempt, paywall_dismissed, trial_started, purchase_completed, purchase_cancelled to PostHog/Mixpanel; correlate with onboarding_step_completed:N events to compute step→paywall→trial funnel.
real_world_examples: 1) Cal AI step 32 of 33 — after AI macro plan reveal at step 30 and mid-onboarding review prompt at 31, paywall fires with 3-day-trial + annual SKU; no close button on first impression. 2) Prayer Lock (Mau Baron, $150K/mo) — after personalized AI prayer reveal, paywall fires with weekly + 3-day-trial; sunk-cost from 5-15min story onboarding. 3) Puff Count — after smoking-cessation pain survey, hard paywall with no skip, framed as 'protecting your quit plan'. 4) Headway (book summaries) — after personalization questionnaire and 'reading speed' demo, hard paywall with annual default, side-by-side after 2026-01 ban (previously toggle). 5) Noom — after BMI calculation, behavioral profile, and 'your weight loss plan' theatrical loader (~7s), paywall with custom plan price anchored in monthly equivalents. 6) Flo — period tracker mandatory trial gate after personalization (height/weight/cycle). 7) Blinkist — after 'reading goals' questionnaire and category preferences, hard paywall with annual default.
mobile_vs_web_diffs: Mobile-primary. Native StoreKit/BillingClient integration via RevenueCat or Adapty SDK. Web equivalent: Stripe Checkout / Paddle modal at end of web-onboarding funnel; web has lower conversion (~0.6-0.8%) but no platform 30% take rate.
platform_design_language_alignment: Liquid Glass (iOS 26+): use translucent material on paywall background; bold left-aligned plan headers; dynamic morphing CTA on selection. M3 Expressive (Android 16+): expressive shape system for plan cards; 4x faster element discovery (18,000-participant study) makes side-by-side card selection (post-toggle-ban) clearer than legacy toggle.
category_fit: STRONG: wellness/habit (Cal AI, Noom, Flo, Calm), AI-wrapper (Prayer Lock, Cal AI, Headway), edtech, fitness, journaling. WEAK / EXCLUDE: productivity B2B (use freemium + persistent CTA instead — see item 37), fintech (KYC must complete first), social network (network effect requires free user mass), utility (Adam Lyttle 4-screen pattern is better fit).
solo_dev_feasibility: 1_week (with RevenueCat Paywalls SDK or Superwall) — dashboard config + remote paywall iteration without app re-submission. 1_month if hand-rolling paywall UI + StoreKit2 / Play Billing v6 directly. Dependencies: RevenueCat free tier ($2.5K MTR cap) or Adapty Pro ($199+/mo) or Superwall (free up to 250 trials/mo).
tooling_options: RevenueCat Paywalls SDK (free up to $2.5K MTR; Pro $199+/mo) — Template 5 modal, server-driven config. Superwall (free <250 trials/mo) — best A/B + onboarding flow integration. Adapty (free <$10K MRR; Pro $99+/mo) — Placement-based remote config + AI paywall builder. Apphud, Qonversion as alternatives. PostHog for funnel + experiment assignment.
ai_dependency: optional. The paywall itself doesn't need AI. The peak that PRECEDES it (AI plan reveal at step N-1) typically does — see items 12, 13.
claude_design_application: Prompt fragment 1: 'Build a hard onboarding paywall in React Native using RevenueCat Paywalls SDK Template 5. Configure: 2 plans side-by-side (annual w/3-day-trial as default, monthly), no close button, no toggle (banned by Apple Guideline 3.1.2 as of 2026-01), explicit timeline disclosure (Day 1 free / Day 4 billed $79.99/year / cancel anytime). Bold left-aligned heading using Liquid Glass alert typography. Forbid: toggle paywalls, fake countdown timers, confirm-shaming on close. Cite RevenueCat State of Subscription Apps 2025 LTV figures in code comments.' Prompt fragment 2: 'Generate the post-AI-reveal paywall transition: 0.7s morph from plan reveal screen into paywall, plans pre-selected based on user signal (questionnaire weight goal → annual / one-off goal → monthly). Use Reanimated 3 LayoutAnimation. Include accessibility: VoiceOver announces plan + price + trial terms; Dynamic Type up to 200%; reduce-motion fallback to crossfade.'
stitch_application: Prompt fragment 1: 'Generate a hard onboarding paywall mockup. Layout: top 30% = headline + value-prop in 24pt bold left-aligned (Liquid Glass style); middle 40% = two side-by-side plan cards (left card = Annual w/3-day-trial, $79.99/yr, badge: Most Popular, default-selected with 2px outline; right card = Monthly $14.99/mo, no badge, 1px outline); bottom 30% = primary CTA Start free trial, fine-print timeline transparency: Day 1 today free / Day 4 billed / Cancel anytime. NO toggle. NO close X visible above the fold. Specify font: Cabinet Grotesk headers, Manrope body. Forbid: Inter, indigo-500, gradient backgrounds, Lucide icons.' Prompt fragment 2: 'Generate a side-by-side plan-selection paywall variant for an AI-wrapper app post-Apple-toggle-ban. 3 cards (Lifetime $129.99, Annual $79.99/yr w/3-day-trial = Most Popular preselect, Monthly $14.99/mo). Each card has its own Start CTA underneath. Hick's Law: max 3 cards.'
ai_codegen_failure_mode: Claude/Cursor/v0/Lovable defaults to: (1) toggle paywall (BANNED 2026-01) — generates 'Free trial enabled' switch above CTA. (2) Indigo-500 gradient background. (3) Inter font. (4) Single-plan layout instead of side-by-side. (5) 'Maybe later' / 'No thanks' close button positioned bottom-center (defeats hard paywall). (6) Hides timeline transparency in tiny gray fine-print. (7) Generates fake countdown timer ('Offer ends in 9:59'). Counter-prompt: 'Forbid toggle paywalls per Apple Guideline 3.1.2 (effective 2026-01). Use side-by-side card selection. Forbid Inter; use Cabinet Grotesk + Manrope. Forbid indigo-500 / purple-blue gradient. Forbid fake countdowns. Mandatory: explicit Day-1/Day-4 timeline disclosure within 48px of CTA. No close button on first impression — see win-back paywall pattern (item 39) for second-chance offer.'
anti_patterns: 1) Toggle paywall (BANNED 2026-01 — instant App Review rejection, Guideline 3.1.2). 2) Hidden trial-to-paid conversion in fine print (Click-to-Cancel rule + DFA flag). 3) Fake countdown timers (DSA Article 25 dark pattern). 4) Confirm-shaming close ('Are you sure you want to give up on yourself?') — DFA Q4 2026 explicit target. 5) Showing paywall with no close on Day 2+ (only first impression earns hard treatment; persistent re-show = sludge). 6) Paywall before AI reveal (loses sunk cost + peak). 7) 5+ plans (Hick's Law violation; Decision Paralysis antipattern). 8) Asymmetric subscribe-vs-cancel (FTC Click-to-Cancel violation, effective 2025-07-14).
regulatory_risk: HIGH. Apple Guideline 3.1.2 (toggle ban, 2026-01) — instant rejection. FTC Click-to-Cancel (effective 2025-07-14) — cancellation must be as easy as signup; onboarding subscription disclosure must mirror cancellation pathway. EU DFA Q4 2026 draft — paywalls with confirm-shaming, fake urgency, asymmetric paths flagged. GDPR — paywall before consent UI is a common compliance break. UK Children's Code — hard paywall to under-18 users without parental consent is non-compliant.
platform_constraints_2026: iOS App Store Guideline 3.1.2 (effective 2026-01-15): TOGGLE PAYWALLS BANNED. User must make a 'clear, affirmative choice' between trial-converts-to-paid and a paid option — implemented as side-by-side cards with explicit per-card CTA, OR explicit timeline transparency where the user actively taps 'Start trial' (not toggles a switch). Guideline 3.1.1: IAP required for digital goods. Guideline 3.1.2: subscription disclosure (price, period, cancellation) must be visible without scrolling. Google Play (Aug 2025): target API 35; disclosure parity required (Play Billing equivalent of toggle ban not formal but Adapty/RC recommend mirroring).
accessibility_implications: WCAG 2.2 AA: 4.5:1 contrast on plan cards (default-selected card needs visible non-color outline). Touch target 24x24px minimum (2.5.8) for plan card tap. VoiceOver/TalkBack: each plan card must announce 'Annual plan, $79.99 per year, includes 3-day free trial, most popular, currently selected'. Dynamic Type up to 200% — plan cards must reflow vertically below ~340pt; never truncate price. Reduce-motion: replace plan-card morph animation with instant fade. No motion-only state indicators (selected card needs both color AND outline AND checkmark).
pitfalls_caveats: 1) Sandbox testing != production — Apple/Google sandbox latency masks real-world timing issues; always test with TestFlight production-like build. 2) Network-flap recovery — paywall must show offline-cached plan SKUs and fall back to 'Try again' (not blank screen). 3) Receipt validation timing — hard paywall close attempt before receipt validates can leave user in zombie state; implement loading-blocked state. 4) Deep-link return from web checkout (item 40) needs explicit re-validation. 5) Gift-recipient flow not currently supported by toggle ban guidance — handle separately. 6) Family-sharing eligibility surfaces in 2026; expose 'Family Sharing included' badge.
cross_cultural_validity: Adapty 2026: localization has 62.3% LTV win rate (highest of any test type). LATAM: aggressive monthly-equivalent anchoring + discount badges ('vs $14.99/mo, save 67%'). JP: social-proof testimonials inline + verifiable trust badges (App Store rating); price-point sensitivity to ¥-rounded values. CN: Apple's auto-converted USD prices feel premium-mismatched; localized pricing strategy required. AR (RTL): mirror entire layout; verify trial-disclosure copy character-counts for VoiceOver in Arabic. US/EU: Apple's auto-conversion routinely sub-optimal; explicit price localization (RC/Adapty offering-per-storefront) required.
prescribed_action: Place a hard paywall (no close button on first impression) immediately after the AI plan reveal at step N-1. Use side-by-side plan-card selection (TWO or THREE cards max; NEVER a toggle — banned by Apple Guideline 3.1.2 as of 2026-01). Pre-select Annual + 3-day-trial as 'Most Popular'. Show timeline transparency within 48px of the primary CTA: 'Today: Free. Day 4: billed $X. Cancel anytime in Settings.' If user dismisses (after first-impression no-close window expires on second mount), fire the win-back paywall (item 39) with a different SKU.
questionnaire_relevance: The paywall is the questionnaire's payoff. Sequencing must build sunk cost (item 26), the AI reveal must precede (item 12), and the questionnaire's identity-questions seed paywall plan defaults (e.g., user picked 'long-term goal' → annual default; 'just trying it' → monthly default).
aha_relevance: Aha precedes paywall by exactly one screen. The peak is engineered upstream (AI reveal); paywall converts that peak into commitment. If the paywall feels like the Aha itself, the funnel is broken — re-architect the reveal upstream.
telemetry_events: paywall_viewed (placement_id, paywall_id, ad_variant, locale), paywall_plan_selected (sku, was_default), paywall_cta_tapped, trial_started (sku, price, currency), purchase_completed (sku, transaction_id, store), paywall_close_attempted, paywall_dismissed, paywall_winback_shown, paywall_winback_converted.
measurement_horizon: Day 0 trial_started + Day 7 trial_to_paid + Day 30 + 12mo cohort LTV. Hard paywalls underperform on Day 0 conversion vs soft (RevenueCat 2025 says soft is +50% Day 0); the 21% LTV lift only appears at 12mo cohort horizon.
emotional_archetype_alignment: STRONG: Magician (transformation framing — 'unlock your plan'), Hero (commitment to journey), Ruler (premium/exclusive), Outlaw (antiestablishment subscription-as-rebellion works for niche apps). WEAK / MISMATCH: Caregiver (hard paywall feels coercive — soft + persistent CTA preferred), Innocent (Apple-clean simplicity — ABC simple plan list better than dramatic).
anti_pattern_paired: Pairs against: Toggle Paywall (banned 2026-01), No-Escape Room (when hard paywall persists past first impression), Decision Paralysis (5+ plans), Confirm-Shaming Exit. Aligns with: AI Plan Reveal (item 12), Sunk Cost questionnaire (item 17), Peak-End engineering (item 6).
sources: [RevenueCat: R.I.P. Toggle Paywall, We Hardly Knew Ye (Jan 2026)](https://www.revenuecat.com/blog/growth/r-i-p-toggle-paywall-we-hardly-knew-ye/)
[Adapty: Your Toggle Paywall Is About To Get Rejected](https://adapty.io/blog/your-toggle-paywall-is-about-to-get-rejected/)
